dict.string:
Usage Coach — an agentic "improving shortcut". It reads a screenshot of your Screen Time, asks a vision model to spot your time sinks, and recommends Shortcuts/automations to reclaim that time. Logs the plan so it gets smarter about you over time.
HOW IT WORKS: 1. (You) Open Settings ▸ Screen Time and take a screenshot first. 2. It grabs your latest screenshot and your goal, sends both to a vision model, and asks for 3 buildable shortcut recipes that would cut your biggest time sinks. 3. It shows the plan and appends it (dated) to a "Usage Coach" note for weekly review.
HONEST BOUNDARY: iOS exposes NO Screen Time / app-usage data to Shortcuts — so this works off a screenshot YOU take. The shortcut never silently watches your usage. And it doesn't "learn" by itself: it logs plans; YOU review them. That's the real self-improving loop.
SETUP: put your model endpoint + key in "Get Contents of URL", OR on iOS 27 swap it for the on-device "Use Model" action (private, no network, no key). Pair it with the Self-review habit — run it weekly.
What do you want more time for this week?
📊 Here's your plan to reclaim time this week — 3 shortcuts to build.
📊 Your week's plan is ready and logged.
